heading: Tribal transportation self-governance program
label: 207 Tribal transportation self-governance program

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: Subject to the requirements of this section, the Secretary shall establish and carry out a program to be known as the tribal transportation self-governance program. The Secretary may delegate responsibilities for administration of the program as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), an Indian tribe shall be eligible to participate in the program if the Indian tribe requests participation in the program by resolution or other official action by the governing body of the Indian tribe, and demonstrates, for the preceding 3 fiscal years, financial stability and financial management capability, and transportation program management capability.

children.heading: .—Criteria for determining financial stability and financial management capacity
children.label: 2 .—Criteria for determining financial stability and financial management capacity
children.text: For the purposes of paragraph (1), evidence that, during the preceding 3 fiscal years, an Indian tribe had no uncorrected significant and material audit exceptions in the required annual audit of the Indian tribe’s self-determination contracts or self-governance funding agreements with any Federal agency shall be conclusive evidence of the required financial stability and financial management capability.

children.heading: .—Criteria for determining transportation program management capability
children.label: 3 .—Criteria for determining transportation program management capability
children.text: The Secretary shall require an Indian tribe to demonstrate transportation program management capability, including the capability to manage and complete projects eligible under this title and projects eligible under chapter 53 of title 49, to gain eligibility for the program.

children.heading: .—Compact required
children.label: 1 .—Compact required
children.text: Upon the request of an eligible Indian tribe, and subject to the requirements of this section, the Secretary shall negotiate and enter into a written compact with the Indian tribe for the purpose of providing for the participation of the Indian tribe in the program.

children.heading: .—Contents
children.label: 2 .—Contents
children.text: A compact entered into under paragraph (1) shall set forth the general terms of the government-to-government relationship between the Indian tribe and the United States under the program and other terms that will continue to apply in future fiscal years.

children.heading: .—Amendments
children.label: 3 .—Amendments
children.text: A compact entered into with an Indian tribe under paragraph (1) may be amended only by mutual agreement of the Indian tribe and the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Funding agreement required
children.label: 1 .—Funding agreement required
children.text: After entering into a compact with an Indian tribe under subsection (c), the Secretary shall negotiate and enter into a written annual funding agreement with the Indian tribe.

children.heading: .—Formula funding and discretionary grants
children.label: i .—Formula funding and discretionary grants
children.text: A funding agreement entered into with an Indian tribe shall authorize the Indian tribe, as determined by the Indian tribe, to plan, conduct, consolidate, administer, and receive full tribal share funding, tribal transit formula funding, and funding to tribes from discretionary and competitive grants administered by the Department for all programs, services, functions, and activities (or portions thereof) that are made available to Indian tribes to carry out tribal transportation programs and programs, services, functions, and activities (or portions thereof) administered by the Secretary that are otherwise available to Indian tribes.

children.heading: .—Inclusion of transferred funds in funding agreement
children.label: I .—Inclusion of transferred funds in funding agreement
children.text: A funding agreement entered into with an Indian tribe shall include Federal-aid funds apportioned to a State under chapter 1 if the State elects to provide a portion of such funds to the Indian tribe for a project eligible under section 202(a). The provisions of this section shall be in addition to the methods for making funding contributions described in section 202(a)(9). Nothing in this section shall diminish the authority of the Secretary to provide funds to an Indian tribe under section 202(a)(9).

children.label: aa
children.text: the transfer may occur in accordance with section 202(a)(9); or

children.label: bb
children.text: the State shall transfer the funds back to the Secretary and the Secretary shall transfer the funds to the Indian tribe in accordance with this section.

children.label: aa
children.text: the State shall not be responsible for constructing or maintaining a project carried out using the funds or for administering or supervising the project or funds during the applicable statute of limitations period related to the construction of the project; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the Indian tribe shall be responsible for constructing and maintaining a project carried out using the funds and for administering and supervising the project and funds in accordance with this section during the applicable statute of limitations period related to the construction of the project.

children.heading: .—Administration of tribal shares
children.label: B .—Administration of tribal shares
children.text: The tribal shares referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be provided without regard to the agency or office of the Department within which the program, service, function, or activity (or portion thereof) is performed.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A funding agreement entered into with an Indian tribe under paragraph (1) shall include provisions pertaining to flexible and innovative financing if agreed upon by the parties.

children.heading: .—Authority to issue regulations
children.label: I .—Authority to issue regulations
children.text: The Secretary may issue regulations to establish the terms and conditions relating to the flexible and innovative financing provisions referred to in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Terms and conditions in absence of regulations
children.label: II .—Terms and conditions in absence of regulations
AA
children.text: section 202(b)(7); and

children.label: BB
children.text: section 202(d)(5), as in effect before the date of enactment of MAP–21 (); orPublic Law 112–141

children.label: bb
children.text: regulations of the Department of the Interior relating to flexible financing contained in part 170 of title 25, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on the date of enactment of the FAST Act.

children.label: A
children.text: terms that generally identify the programs, services, functions, and activities (or portions thereof) to be performed or administered by the Indian tribe; and

children.label: i
children.text: the general budget category assigned;

children.label: ii
children.text: the funds to be provided, including those funds to be provided on a recurring basis;

children.label: iii
children.text: the time and method of transfer of the funds;

children.label: iv
children.text: the responsibilities of the Secretary and the Indian tribe; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other provision agreed to by the Indian tribe and the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Applicability of existing agreement
children.label: A .—Applicability of existing agreement
children.text: Absent notification from an Indian tribe that the Indian tribe is withdrawing from or retroceding the operation of 1 or more programs, services, functions, or activities (or portions thereof) identified in a funding agreement, or unless otherwise agreed to by the parties, each funding agreement shall remain in full force and effect until a subsequent funding agreement is executed.

children.heading: .—Effective date of subsequent agreement
children.label: B .—Effective date of subsequent agreement
children.text: The terms of the subsequent funding agreement shall be retroactive to the end of the term of the preceding funding agreement.

children.heading: .—Consent of indian tribe required
children.label: 5 .—Consent of indian tribe required
children.text: The Secretary shall not revise, amend, or require additional terms in a new or subsequent funding agreement without the consent of the Indian tribe that is subject to the agreement unless such terms are required by Federal law.

children.label: i
children.text: redesign or consolidate programs, services, functions, and activities (or portions thereof) included in a funding agreement; and

children.label: I
children.text: expended on projects identified in a transportation improvement program approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: aa
children.text: appropriations Acts;

children.label: bb
children.text: this title and chapter 53 of title 49; and

children.label: cc
children.text: any other applicable law.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: B .—Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), if, pursuant to subsection (d), an Indian tribe receives a discretionary or competitive grant from the Secretary or receives State apportioned funds, the Indian tribe shall use the funds for the purpose for which the funds were originally authorized.

children.heading: .—Authority of indian tribes
children.label: i .—Authority of indian tribes
children.text: An Indian tribe may retrocede (fully or partially) to the Secretary programs, services, functions, or activities (or portions thereof) included in a compact or funding agreement.

children.label: I
children.text: reassume the remaining funding associated with the retroceded programs, functions, services, and activities (or portions thereof) included in the applicable compact or funding agreement;

children.label: II
children.text: out of such remaining funds, transfer funds associated with Department of Interior programs, services, functions, or activities (or portions thereof) to the Secretary of the Interior to carry out transportation services provided by the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: III
children.text: distribute funds not transferred under subclause (II) in accordance with applicable law.

children.heading: .—Correction of programs
children.label: iii .—Correction of programs
children.text: If the Secretary makes a finding under subsection (f)(2)(B) and no funds are available under subsection (f)(2)(A)(ii), the Secretary shall not be required to provide additional funds to complete or correct any programs, functions, services, or activities (or portions thereof).

children.label: I
children.text: 1 year after the date of submission of the request; or

children.label: II
children.text: the date on which the funding agreement expires; or

children.label: ii
children.text: such date as may be mutually agreed upon by the parties and, with respect to Department of the Interior programs, functions, services, and activities (or portions thereof), the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: by an official of the Department who holds a position at a higher organizational level within the Department than the level of the departmental agency in which the decision that is the subject of the appeal was made; or

children.label: B
children.text: by an administrative judge.

children.heading: Termination of compact or funding agreement.—
children.label: 2 Termination of compact or funding agreement.—

children.heading: .—Provision to be included in compact or funding agreement
children.label: i .—Provision to be included in compact or funding agreement
I
children.text: terminate the compact or funding agreement (or a portion thereof); and

children.label: II
children.text: reassume the remaining funding associated with the reassumed programs, functions, services, and activities included in the compact or funding agreement.

children.heading: .—Transfers of funds
children.label: ii .—Transfers of funds
children.text: Out of any funds reassumed under clause (i)(II), the Secretary may transfer the funds associated with Department of the Interior programs, functions, services, and activities (or portions thereof) to the Secretary of the Interior to provide continued transportation services in accordance with applicable law.

children.label: i
children.text: imminent jeopardy to a trust asset, natural resources, or public health and safety that is caused by an act or omission of the Indian tribe and that arises out of a failure to carry out the compact or funding agreement, as determined by the Secretary; or

children.label: ii
children.text: gross mismanagement with respect to funds or programs transferred to the Indian tribe under the compact or funding agreement, as determined by the Secretary in consultation with the Inspector General of the Department, as appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary has first provided written notice and a hearing on the record to the Indian tribe that is subject to the compact or funding agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Indian tribe has not taken corrective action to remedy the mismanagement of funds or programs or the imminent jeopardy to a trust asset, natural resource, or public health and safety.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary makes a finding of imminent substantial and irreparable jeopardy to a trust asset, natural resource, or public health and safety; and

children.label: II
children.text: the jeopardy arises out of a failure to carry out the compact or funding agreement.

children.heading: .—Hearings
children.label: ii .—Hearings
children.text: If the Secretary terminates a compact or funding agreement (or portion thereof) under clause (i), the Secretary shall provide the Indian tribe subject to the compact or agreement with a hearing on the record not later than 10 days after the date of such termination.

children.heading: .—Burden of proof
children.label: E .—Burden of proof
children.text: In any hearing or appeal involving a decision to terminate a compact or funding agreement (or portion thereof) under this paragraph, the Secretary shall have the burden of proof in demonstrating by clear and convincing evidence the validity of the grounds for the termination.

children.heading: .—Cost Principles
children.label: g .—Cost Principles
children.text: In administering funds received under this section, an Indian tribe shall apply cost principles under the applicable Office of Management and Budget circular, except as modified by section 106 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (), other provisions of law, or by any exemptions to applicable Office of Management and Budget circulars subsequently granted by the Office of Management and Budget. No other audit or accounting standards shall be required by the Secretary. Any claim by the Federal Government against the Indian tribe relating to funds received under a funding agreement based on any audit conducted pursuant to this subsection shall be subject to the provisions of section 106(f) of that Act ().25 U.S.C. 532525 U.S.C. 5325(f)

the sum of the funding that the Indian tribe would otherwise receive for the program, function, service, or activity in accordance with a funding formula or other allocation method established under this title or chapter 53 of title 49; and
such additional amounts as the Secretary determines equal the amounts that would have been withheld for the costs of the Bureau of Indian Affairs for administration of the program or project.

children.heading: .—Standards
children.label: 1 .—Standards
children.text: Construction projects carried out under programs administered by an Indian tribe with funds transferred to the Indian tribe pursuant to a funding agreement entered into under this section shall be constructed pursuant to the construction program standards set forth in applicable regulations or as specifically approved by the Secretary (or the Secretary’s designee).

children.heading: .—Monitoring
children.label: 2 .—Monitoring
children.text: Construction programs shall be monitored by the Secretary in accordance with applicable regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: the inclusion of programs, services, functions, and activities (or portions thereof) and funds associated therewith, in compacts and funding agreements; and

children.label: B
children.text: the implementation of the compacts and funding agreements.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: An Indian tribe may submit to the Secretary a written request to waive application of a regulation promulgated under this section with respect to a compact or funding agreement. The request shall identify the regulation sought to be waived and the basis for the request.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of receipt of a written request under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall approve or deny the request in writing.

children.heading: .—Review
children.label: ii .—Review
children.text: The Secretary shall review any application by an Indian tribe for a waiver bearing in mind increasing opportunities for using flexible policy approaches at the Indian tribal level.

children.heading: .—Deemed approval
children.label: iii .—Deemed approval
children.text: If the Secretary does not approve or deny a request submitted under subparagraph (A) on or before the last day of the 90-day period referred to in clause (i), the request shall be deemed approved.

children.heading: .—Denials
children.label: iv .—Denials
children.text: If the application for a waiver is not granted, the agency shall provide the applicant with the reasons for the denial as part of the written response required in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Finality of decisions
children.label: v .—Finality of decisions
children.text: A decision by the Secretary under this subparagraph shall be final for the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: maintain current tribal transportation program funding agreements and program agreements; or

children.label: B
children.text: enter into new agreements under the authority of section 202(b)(7).

children.heading: .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.label: 2 .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to impair or diminish the authority of the Secretary under section 202(b)(7).

children.heading: .—Applicability of Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act
children.label: l .—Applicability of Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act

children.text: Subsections (a), (b), (d), (g), and (h) of section 506 of such Act (), relating to general provisions.25 U.S.C. 5386
Subsections (b) through (e) and (g) of section 507 of such Act (), relating to provisions relating to the Secretary of Health and Human Services.25 U.S.C. 5387
Subsections (a), (b), (d), (e), (g), (h), (i), and (k) of section 508 of such Act (), relating to transfer of funds.25 U.S.C. 5388
Section 510 of such Act (), relating to Federal procurement laws and regulations.25 U.S.C. 5390
Section 511 of such Act (), relating to civil actions.25 U.S.C. 5391

Subsections (a)(1), (a)(2), and (c) through (f) of section 512 of such Act (), relating to facilitation, except that subsection (c)(1) of that section shall be applied by substituting “transportation facilities and other facilities” for “school buildings, hospitals, and other facilities”.25 U.S.C. 5392
Subsections (a) and (b) of section 515 of such Act (), relating to disclaimers.25 U.S.C. 5395
Subsections (a) and (b) of section 516 of such Act (), relating to application of title I provisions.25 U.S.C. 5396
Section 518 of such Act (), relating to appeals.25 U.S.C. 5398

children.heading: .—Compact
children.label: A .—Compact
children.text: The term “compact” means a compact between the Secretary and an Indian tribe entered into under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Department
children.label: B .—Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Eligible indian tribe
children.label: C .—Eligible indian tribe
children.text: The term “eligible Indian tribe” means an Indian tribe that is eligible to participate in the program, as determined under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Funding agreement
children.label: D .—Funding agreement
children.text: The term “funding agreement” means a funding agreement between the Secretary and an Indian tribe entered into under subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Indian tribe
children.label: E .—Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, or community that is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians. In any case in which an Indian tribe has authorized another Indian tribe, an intertribal consortium, or a tribal organization to plan for or carry out programs, services, functions, or activities (or portions thereof) on its behalf under this section, the authorized Indian tribe, intertribal consortium, or tribal organization shall have the rights and responsibilities of the authorizing Indian tribe (except as otherwise provided in the authorizing resolution or in this title). In such event, the term “Indian tribe” as used in this section shall include such other authorized Indian tribe, intertribal consortium, or tribal organization.

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: F .—Program
children.text: The term “program” means the tribal transportation self-governance program established under this section.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: G .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Transportation programs
children.label: H .—Transportation programs
children.text: The term “transportation programs” means all programs administered or financed by the Department under this title and chapter 53 of title 49.

children.heading: .—Applicability of other definitions
children.label: 2 .—Applicability of other definitions
children.text: In this section, the definitions set forth in sections 4 and 501 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (; 5381) apply, except as otherwise expressly provided in this section.25 U.S.C. 5304

children.heading: .—Promulgation
children.label: A .—Promulgation
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the FAST Act, the Secretary shall initiate procedures under subchapter III of chapter 5 of title 5 to negotiate and promulgate such regulations as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Publication of proposed regulations
children.label: B .—Publication of proposed regulations
children.text: Proposed regulations to implement this section shall be published in the Federal Register by the Secretary not later than 42 months after such date of enactment.

children.heading: .—Expiration of authority
children.label: C .—Expiration of authority
children.text: The authority to promulgate regulations under subparagraph (A) shall expire 48 months after such date of enactment.

children.heading: .—Extension of deadlines
children.label: D .—Extension of deadlines
children.text: A deadline set forth in subparagraph (B) or (C) may be extended up to 180 days if the negotiated rulemaking committee referred to in paragraph (2) concludes that the committee cannot meet the deadline and the Secretary so notifies the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A negotiated rulemaking committee established pursuant to to carry out this subsection shall have as its members only Federal and tribal government representatives, a majority of whom shall be nominated by and be representatives of Indian tribes with funding agreements under this title.section 565 of title 5

children.heading: .—Requirements
children.label: B .—Requirements
children.text: The committee shall confer with, and accommodate participation by, representatives of Indian tribes, inter-tribal consortia, tribal organizations, and individual tribal members.

children.heading: .—Adaptation of procedures
children.label: C .—Adaptation of procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall adapt the negotiated rulemaking procedures to the unique context of self-governance and the government-to-government relationship between the United States and Indian tribes.

children.heading: Effect.—
children.label: 3 Effect.—
children.text: The lack of promulgated regulations shall not limit the effect of this section.

children.heading: .—Effect of circulars, policies, manuals, guidance, and rules
children.label: 4 .—Effect of circulars, policies, manuals, guidance, and rules
children.text: Unless expressly agreed to by the participating Indian tribe in the compact or funding agreement, the participating Indian tribe shall not be subject to any agency circular, policy, manual, guidance, or rule adopted by the Department, except regulations promulgated under this section.
